title: Prove source correlation through the official WordPress production bundle
description:
## Product/Profile Scope
Project the authenticated Genes map and SDK-034 lookup contract through the exact SDK-033 @wordpress/scripts/webpack entry and production modes used by wp70-release.

## Source Authority
PRD Gate G2; ADR-013; ADR-014; exact SDK-033 build-tool lock and SDK-034 source-correlation contract.

## Layer Owner
WordPress build adapter and package evidence layer; generic Genes map defects remain upstream Genes work.

## Generated Artifacts Affected
Official development and minified WordPress bundles, layered or composed Source Map v3 files, source index records, debug companion, and trace receipts.

## Real Runtime Evidence Required
Deliberate development and production browser failures from the exact Gutenberg entry must resolve to the same expected Haxe token in real Chromium, with dependency extraction and asset metadata unchanged.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
Treating the bounded esbuild fixture as proof for webpack/@wordpress/scripts, guessing across filenames, silently accepting partial composition, or shipping source content and machine-local paths.

## Stop Condition
Retain and authenticate an explicit JS-to-TSX-to-Haxe two-stage chain for the affected entry/mode when correct full composition cannot be proven; never label it composed.
design: Reuse the SDK-034 Source Map v3 reader, source index, trace CLI, integrity/path rules, and packaging policy. Admission remains exact-profile + exact-adapter + exact-entry + exact-mode. If @wordpress/scripts changes layer identity, normalize in the WordPress adapter rather than adding WordPress knowledge to Genes or weakening map validation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Run the exact SDK-033 @wordpress/scripts development and production builds from immutable locks.
- Independently validate the Genes layer and every final bundler map layer.
- Map real development and minified Chromium throws to the same expected Haxe source token, or ship an authenticated two-stage result.
- Prove maps, source indexes, and Haxe/source content are absent from the default production ZIP and path/secret negatives fail closed.
- Preserve final dependency handles, *.asset.php, translations, public exports, and deterministic output from SDK-033.
- Record local and hosted receipts without broadening the claim to unrelated entries, modes, or adapters.
notes: Completed on 2026-07-18. Exact @wordpress/scripts 31.5.0 / Webpack 5.108.4 development and production bundles compose through authenticated Source Map v3 to EditorPanel.hx:12:8 in real Chromium on linux/arm64 145.0.7632.0 and linux/amd64 145.0.7632.6; deterministic production plugin ZIP excludes maps/index/source content and the separate debug companion is content-bound. Full WordPress 7.0/MySQL runtime, PHP 7.4/8.4 syntax, tamper/privacy/ambiguity negatives, SDK-031, SDK-032, SDK-025, and SDK-034 regressions pass. No Genes source change or PR was required. Initial hosted run 29651610543 at commit 9d048df2b05d1ad30bbd6bd06d7efefcd23ebeab passed nine of ten jobs; wordpress-runtime job 88098774931 failed before the G2.4 build because the clean runner validated the immutable Genes cache coordinate before project-scoped lix download materialized it. Commit c7f01b8a0514ac3873176c1be26f738193234b40 fixed the generalized boundary by downloading authenticated Lix dependencies before exact-path validation, mounting independently resolved library-cache and stdlib roots read-only into the pinned Node container, and recognizing immutable Genes/stdlib suffixes without assuming a machine-local cache parent. A fresh isolated-cache proof and complete local build-only gate passed. Hosted run 29652492122 then passed all ten jobs; wordpress-runtime job 88101074575 emitted linux/amd64 browser receipt 413b0d328099a29f5e1a273c384d88e3a37e5450bbb98b4b7181b990dafe635d with all platform-independent artifact hashes equal to the ARM64 proof. G2.4, SDK-033, G0, and architecture receipts are promoted to verified; publication and general production support remain explicitly unclaimed.
close_reason: Verified exact official @wordpress/scripts development and minified production source correlation to EditorPanel.hx:12:8 in real ARM64 and hosted AMD64 Chromium, with deterministic native WordPress assets, privacy-safe production packaging, fail-closed mutations, and green ten-job hosted run 29652492122 at c7f01b8a0514ac3873176c1be26f738193234b40. No Genes change or PR was required.

title: Harden Git hooks and leak guards before public repository creation
description:
## Product/Profile Scope
Repository security and contribution workflow before the SDK is published; no compiler or WordPress product behavior.

## Source Authority
The maintainer's public-repository authorization, AGENTS.md, SECURITY.md, and the reviewed hook patterns in ../haxe.c and ../haxe.ruby.

## Layer Owner
SDK repository security and operations governance.

## Generated Artifacts Affected
Tracked Git hook shims, hook installer, formatter and Gitleaks guards, decoded Beads scan wrapper, CI workflow, and contributor documentation.

## Real Runtime Evidence Required
Exercise the installed pre-commit and pre-push chains, exact formatter/version checks, staged and full-history Gitleaks scans, machine-local-path rejection, decoded Beads current/history scan, and clean repository checks.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
Publishing secrets, tokens, private paths, or sensitive Beads history; bypassing Beads hooks; silently formatting third-party bytes; or weakening scans through broad allowlists.

## Acceptance Commands/Receipts
Run hook installer, hook self-tests, repository checks, compiler tests, full-history and decoded-Beads scans, workflow syntax review, beads lint/cycles/orphans, and a clean commit through the real pre-commit hook.

## Dependencies
Must complete before SDK-004 creates and pushes the public Git and Dolt remotes.

## Stop Condition
Do not create the public GitHub repository or push any Git/Dolt ref until every local leak scan passes without broad allowlisting.
acceptance_criteria: Tracked pre-commit and pre-push hooks preserve Beads integration, require exact Haxe Formatter 1.18.0 and Gitleaks >=8.30.0, format staged repository-owned Haxe, scan staged changes and full Git history with redaction, reject staged machine-local paths, scan decoded current and historical Beads records before Dolt push, run in public CI, install reproducibly, and pass positive and negative self-tests before any public remote is created.
close_reason: Verified tracked Formatter/Gitleaks/path hooks, public CI equivalents, decoded Beads guard, adversarial canaries, and clean full-history scans before remote creation.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Pre-public verification passed: hook installer configured relative core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks; hook self-test accepted safe content and rejected both machine-local-path and synthetic high-entropy API-key canaries; Formatter 1.18.0 checked 5 Haxe files; reflaxe.php Haxe 4.3.7/PHP runtime fixture passed; Gitleaks 8.30.0 found no leaks in 5 reachable commits or staged bytes; decoded scan found no secrets or machine-local paths across current state and complete history for 105 Beads records; repository checks, bd lint, and dependency-cycle checks passed. Workflow actions are full-SHA pinned and the Linux Gitleaks archive is pinned by reviewed SHA-256.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Real tracked pre-commit and pre-push chains passed on commit eea231c; every reachable committed revision remained clean after the hook implementation itself was added.

title: SDK-004: Configure canonical Git and beads remotes
description:
## Product/Profile Scope
Repository transport for the independent SDK and beads data; no product/profile implementation.

## Source Authority
AGENTS.md session-close protocol, bd sync architecture, and the canonical hosting repository chosen by the maintainer.

## Layer Owner
SDK repository/operations governance.

## Generated Artifacts Affected
Git remote configuration, default/upstream branch relationship, beads Dolt remote ref, CI repository context, and push verification receipt.

## Real Runtime Evidence Required
A clean non-interactive `git pull --rebase`, `git push`, `bd dolt push`, remote ref inspection, and final status showing local code and tracker state synchronized.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
Guessing the wrong repository, exposing credentials, overwriting an unrelated remote, or pushing unpublished/licensing-blocked artifacts to a public destination.

## Acceptance Commands/Receipts
Record the maintainer-approved remote URL/visibility, non-secret authentication method, exact pushed commit, Dolt ref result, default branch, and final `git status`/remote comparison.

## Dependencies
Discovered while closing SDK-000; depends on the independent repository bootstrap.

## Stop Condition
Do not invent a remote URL, change repository visibility, or publish externally without maintainer-authorized destination and credentials.
acceptance_criteria:
- A maintainer-authorized canonical Git remote and visibility are recorded.
- The local branch has a verified upstream without embedding credentials in repository files.
- `git pull --rebase`, `git push`, and `bd dolt push` succeed non-interactively.
- Local code commit and beads data refs are confirmed present remotely; final status is synchronized.
notes:
Sync attempt after local commit 044ad13:
- `git remote -v`: no remotes.
- `git pull --rebase`: exit 1, no tracking information for master.
- `git push`: exit 128, no configured push destination.
- `bd dolt push`: skipped because no Dolt remote is configured.
Resolution requires maintainer-authorized repository URL/visibility and authentication; no destination was guessed.
Sync attempt after local commit 4fda6fc on 2026-07-16: git pull --rebase exited 1 because master has no tracking branch; git push exited 128 because no push destination exists; bd dolt push skipped successfully because no Dolt remote is configured. No destination was guessed. Commit 4fda6fc remains local pending maintainer-authorized Git and Dolt remotes.
close_reason: Created the authorized public GitHub repository, established main/origin and SSH-backed Dolt transport, published and verified both refs, recorded canonical metadata, and passed local plus hosted security/toolchain gates.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Maintainer-authorized public destination created with GitHub CLI: https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/wordpresshx. Git origin uses HTTPS without embedded credentials; default branch is main; initial published code commit was f8556af69e5b12ad34093fbed63a8e71acd72865. Beads origin is configured as git+https://github.com/fullofcaffeine/wordpresshx.git for refs/dolt/data. Canonical receipt and compiler destination metadata are being committed before final Git/Dolt synchronization.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: The decoded Beads scan passed on both attempts. The initial git+https Dolt push failed because Dolt did not reuse the GitHub CLI HTTPS credential helper; it published no data ref. Existing SSH authentication was verified non-interactively, the Beads remote was changed to git+ssh://git@github.com/fullofcaffeine/wordpresshx.git, and the audited retry succeeded. Remote refs now advertise main at 2ffb4e5646074cc11964e81e9db3f68e83b14f3a and refs/dolt/data at 99af870696dfbd1b01dff7f3949aa6ac0fff9578. Hosted CI run 29549193291 passed; GitHub secret scanning and push protection are enabled.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Final canonical-metadata push reached main at a4110781a2572b887679111d0f419394319f0aa2 through the repository pre-push guards; hosted CI run 29549410074 completed successfully.

title: SDK-111: Prove a complete Haxe-only WordPress site and native deployment
description:
## Product/Profile Scope
Post-MVP complete example combining Haxe-first theme, companion modules/shared contracts, design tokens, and independent deployment artifacts.

## Source Authority
PRD §§15, 20.2 modes 7–8, 25.3; native theme/plugin runtime and workspace contracts.

## Layer Owner
SDK example/theme/workspace.

## Generated Artifacts Affected
Theme/plugin/block sources, generated native artifacts, independent ZIPs, deployment manifest/checksums, docs, visual/accessibility evidence, and provenance.

## Real Runtime Evidence Required
Fresh workspace build; independent install/activation/update; hierarchy/editor/frontend/visual/accessibility/security/performance; remove/rollback module.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
Presenting a site builder or theme preview as MVP support.

## Acceptance Commands/Receipts
Run the named clean-environment/runtime/browser/security/performance/package/release checks; record exact commands, immutable input/tool/artifact hashes, raw logs/results, reviewers, and evidence-ledger IDs. Expected dependency refs: 084, 110.

## Dependencies
Canonical blocking edges are represented directly in beads and must match PRD §27.2 plus applicable ADRs.

## Stop Condition
Keep post-MVP until theme and workspace foundations independently pass.
acceptance_criteria:
- Use only public SDK APIs to author the reference site with zero handwritten PHP, JS/TS, WordPress JSON metadata, or CSS configuration.
- Use typed Haxe/HXX for site composition, server/browser behavior, native hierarchy templates, blocks, admin UI, metadata, design tokens, and generated styles.
- Emit independent normal theme/plugin/block packages with no Haxe, HXX, CLI, or proprietary runtime on the server.
- Pass clean scaffold/build, exact package install/update, hierarchy/editor/frontend, visual/accessibility, security, performance, removal, rollback, and source-correlation evidence.
notes: Stable-product acceptance vertical: the reference site must use public SDK APIs with zero handwritten PHP, JS/TS, WordPress JSON metadata, or CSS configuration. HXX supplies theme/admin/block markup; typed Haxe supplies behavior, metadata, design tokens, styles, and composition; the build emits ordinary independently installable theme/plugin/block packages. Optional static assets and explicit external interop fixtures must be clearly separated.

## Acceptance Commands/Receipts
Run the named clean-environment/runtime/browser/security/performance/package/release checks; record exact commands, immutable input/tool/artifact hashes, raw logs/results, reviewers, and evidence-ledger IDs. Expected dependency refs: 045, 101.

Canonical blocking edges are represented directly in beads and must match PRD §27.2 plus applicable ADRs.

notes: Required full-site product path: Site.hx/module declarations are the authoring authority and the root JSON/deployment manifests are generated or CLI-managed projections. The workspace must build a complete site without requiring handwritten PHP, JS/TS, WordPress JSON metadata, or CSS configuration, while preserving independent native deployables.

description:

Three consecutive fresh RC runs, external consumer review, independent readability/ownership/claims review, no P0/P1, all blocking cells green.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk

title: SDK-080: Accept HXX architecture and parser dependency decision
description:

title: ADR-021: Release and support policy
description:

SDK 0.x through 1.0 and exact profiles.

RC rehearsal, patch/rollback exercise, version/claim matrix review.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
A stable claim without maintainer capacity or a finite supported matrix.

## Acceptance Commands/Receipts

## Dependencies
Blocking edges and affected implementation beads are represented directly in beads.

## Stop Condition

## Decision
Open until this bead is accepted. The ADR must resolve: Stable definition, exact support windows, deprecations, security patches, channels, owners, and rollback.

## Rationale
The PRD makes this decision explicit before dependent implementation because a stable claim without maintainer capacity or a finite supported matrix.

## Alternatives Considered
At minimum, compare the PRD recommendation, deferring or omitting the capability, and the strongest credible alternative architecture found during review. Record evidence, tradeoffs, and why each non-selected option was rejected or limited.

## Consequences

acceptance_criteria:
- Define nightly/preview/stable criteria.
- Define WordPress/PHP/Node/browser/DB/profile windows and deprecation rules.
- Define security response, patch/backport, release, and rollback ownership.
notes:
Decision direction: keep all 0.x releases nightly/preview and unsupported; admit stable only at 1.0+ after G8, licensing, exact production-supported claim/matrix evidence, tested private security intake, and named primary/backup ownership. Use one finite release-specific support table with a default 180-day term that cannot be shortened after publication; exact profiles and runtime/toolchain versions only, never ranges by implication. Stable public deprecations remain for at least one minor and 180 days and are removed only in a major except additive security withdrawal. Current accountable product/release/rollback/compatibility owner is Marcelo Serpa; backup and tested security channel remain unassigned/disabled and therefore block stable release.
Accepted policy record docs/adr/021-release-and-support-policy.md: 0.x nightly/preview only; stable starts no earlier than 1.0/G8 and requires exact production-supported manifests, licensing authorization, tested private security intake, exercised primary/backup ownership, final-byte release/rollback evidence, and explicit capacity acceptance. Future stable minor support is a non-shortenable release-specific 180-day term; exact profile/runtime/toolchain identities only; stable deprecations require one subsequent minor plus 180 days and normally a major for removal. Current private vulnerability reporting is disabled and backup ownership unassigned, so this decision creates no present support claim.
close_reason: Accepted finite exact-matrix release/support policy with explicit current stable blockers and no present support claim.

notes:
Accepted 2026-07-16 by the project owner direction captured in this session, with Codex architecture/implementation review.

Decision artifact: docs/adr/001-product-and-repository-boundary.md

Resolved:
- wordpress-hx-sdk is an independent product/repository/tracker/release authority.
- Full-port Core linking, original-path replacement, ownership progression, and distribution machinery remain full-port-only.

## Acceptance Commands/Receipts
Record exact commands, immutable input hashes, artifact hashes, environment matrix, and evidence IDs on closure.

## Dependencies
Canonical blocking edges are represented directly in beads; hierarchy is organizational only.

## Stop Condition
Narrow the browser promise before block breadth if strict native integration cannot be maintained.
acceptance_criteria:

- Final bundle dependency/asset metadata matches native tooling.
- Runtime, accessibility, keyboard, source-map, and ordinary-JS export fixtures pass.

Canonical blocking edges are represented directly in beads; hierarchy is organizational only.

title: Gate G0: Product authority and baseline lock
description:

title: Assess and retire exact WP 7.0 build-tool advisories
description:
## Risk
The provider-exact SDK-033 build graph pins @wordpress/scripts 31.5.0 and @wordpress/components 32.2.0. An npm audit of the exact lock on 2026-07-18 reported 36 development/build-only findings (1 low, 23 moderate, 12 high, 0 critical), including transitive tooling paths. Blind audit fixes would replace the exact WordPress provider and invalidate the compatibility profile.

## Scope
Reproduce and classify each advisory against the bounded, containerized build path; determine whether an official compatible provider update retires it; supersede the profile through an ADR when required. Do not ship node_modules or build tooling.

## Mitigations Until Retirement
Use an immutable container digest and package lock, npm ci --ignore-scripts, an unprivileged user, a disposable workspace/cache, no untrusted project inputs, no dev server/network exposure, and keep publication blocked by existing gates.

## Source Authority
Provider package manifests and lock, npm advisory records, official WordPress package releases, SDK-033 evidence receipt.
design: Treat exact-provider compatibility and supply-chain remediation as separate explicit claims. Never run npm audit fix or float versions inside a frozen profile. A provider upgrade requires regenerated evidence and full browser/runtime regression gates.
acceptance_criteria: Record the exact advisory inventory and reachability analysis; prove the isolated no-lifecycle-script build; identify and test an official non-breaking provider update or approve a superseding profile ADR; update locks/receipts without weakening dependency parity; retain zero critical findings.

title: SDK-116: Build Haxe WooCommerce reference on WordPress and Next.js
description:

## Subject/Audience/Job
Morrow Workshop, a fictional precision hand-tool shop for repair-minded makers; help a buyer compare exact specifications and complete a purchase confidently.

Exact admitted official WooCommerce artifact and public Store/REST APIs, WordPress core, core Gutenberg/WooCommerce blocks that the exact profile proves, and SDK-113 shared contracts.

## Layer Owner

## Real Runtime Evidence Required
Catalog/product/variation/inventory updates; native shop/product/cart/checkout; Next catalog/product/cart/checkout; session/cookie/nonce behavior; order through an offline test payment method; cache/webhook update; accessibility, visual, security, performance, and removal.

## Stop Condition
Do not use proprietary extensions, live payment credentials, floating WooCommerce versions, guessed Store API contracts, or a custom commerce kernel.

## Required Skills
frontend-design
design: Use a precision workbench rather than a generic luxury storefront: graphite, machine aluminum, blueprint blue, bone-white labels, and a single safety-orange action color. The signature is a product-spec rail that behaves like a physical measuring jig and is populated from actual typed attributes. Pair a distinctive technical display face with a calm book face for instructions and a tabular utility face for dimensions/prices. Motion should explain assembly or cart state, not decorate.
acceptance_criteria:
- Author catalog projections, routes, product/spec components, cart/checkout flows, metadata, tokens, styling, and interactions in Haxe/HXX only.
- Use only the exact SDK-117-admitted official WooCommerce distribution plus WordPress core; require no proprietary extension or remote SaaS.

title: SDK-113: Implement reusable WordPress content and NextJsHx adapter surface
description:
## Product/Profile Scope
Create the reusable Haxe surface shared by native WordPress and Next.js examples: canonical content/domain contracts, exact WordPress transport clients, typed core Gutenberg block trees, design tokens, cache/invalidation events, and NextJsHx renderer/adapters.

## Source Authority
Exact WordPress profile schemas and runtime; core Gutenberg block metadata/serialization; exact pinned public NextJsHx and Genes packages; provider locks admitted by SDK-117.

## Layer Owner

## Generated Artifacts Affected
Haxe declarations, PHP adapters, REST or optional provider schemas, strict TS/TSX, App Router files, core-block unions/renderers, CSS/token projections, manifests, maps, and fixtures.

## Real Runtime Evidence Required
Native WordPress authoring/rendering; headless fetch and preview; Next production SSR/RSC/client islands; cache tags and revalidation; supported core-block parity; unknown-block and unsafe-HTML negatives.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
Inventing a CMS abstraction that erases WordPress semantics, copying NextJsHx internals, floating sibling paths, broad dynamic JSON, unsanitized rendered HTML, or assuming every Gutenberg block is portable.

## Stop Condition
Support a smaller explicit block/provider surface when exact types, serialization, sanitization, cache behavior, or renderer parity cannot be proven.
design: Expose WordPress-native concepts directly: posts, pages, terms, media, menus, block trees, revisions/previews, and provider capabilities. Generate a closed supported core-block union from the exact profile. Render that union through small backend adapters: native WordPress markup/HXX and NextJsHx React HXX. Unknown blocks must be omitted with diagnostics or enter an explicit sanitized-rendered escape hatch. Core REST is the baseline; WPGraphQL is an optional provider candidate, never the canonical domain model. Depend on an immutable released NextJsHx contract when available; until then keep integration fixtures pinned and non-publishable, with no ../nextjshx runtime path.
acceptance_criteria:

- Generate exact profile-specific core Gutenberg block attribute unions and typed constructors for the supported subset, including nested blocks and explicit unknown-block policy.
- Provide native WordPress and NextJsHx rendering adapters with common props/children/design-token contracts but explicit target semantics.
- Use typed codecs for WordPress REST and optional admitted providers; forbid broad Dynamic and implicit trusted HTML.
- Prove preview, draft/published, pagination, media, cache tags/revalidation, errors, and source correlation on real WordPress plus a clean Next production build.
- Consume only immutable public NextJsHx/Genes identities; route generalized upstream gaps through isolated worktrees, full regression suites, and PRs.

title: SDK-117: Enforce open-source WordPress provider trust and provenance lock
description:
## Product/Profile Scope
Define the fail-closed admission policy and immutable lock for third-party WordPress plugins or libraries used by maintained examples.

## Source Authority
Canonical upstream repositories, WordPress.org metadata, release artifacts, SPDX/license texts, published security advisories, and exact runtime behavior.

## Layer Owner
SDK provider governance and example dependency locks.

## Generated Artifacts Affected
Provider trust policy/schema, exact-version/source/artifact lock, capability declarations, SBOM/notices inputs, audit report, and real-provider receipts.

## Real Runtime Evidence Required
Install exact artifacts on the supported WordPress profile; prove present, absent, wrong-version, conditional-capability, update, removal, and compromised/mismatched artifact negatives.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
Equating popularity with safety, accepting source-available proprietary code, floating latest versions, silently trusting transitive services, or advertising an unaudited provider as certified.

## Stop Condition
Exclude a provider when identity, license, source, artifact hash, maintenance ownership, advisory posture, or required runtime behavior cannot be verified.
design: Start with WordPress core and core Gutenberg, which require no optional plugin. Evaluate a minimal provider set for the examples; likely candidates are an official WordPress GraphQL implementation for the blog and official WooCommerce for commerce, but no candidate is admitted until current authoritative evidence is locked. Keep trust conclusions dated, scoped, reproducible, and distinct from a security guarantee.
acceptance_criteria:
- Define machine-validated admission fields for canonical source, OSI-compatible license evidence, exact release/version, artifact checksum, maintainer identity, supported WordPress/PHP profile, advisory review date, capabilities, and removal path.
- Fail on floating versions, hash mismatch, missing source/license, stale or unresolved critical advisory evidence, unexpected network/service dependency, and undeclared transitive runtime code.
- Distinguish inventoried, contract-tested, real-provider-tested, and production-supported states without calling any provider universally safe.
- Generate SBOM/notices inputs and capability tokens from the same lock.
- Prove positive and negative installation/runtime fixtures on exact WordPress 7.0 lanes.

title: Reconcile WPHX PHP source-receipt drift before the next compiler import
description:
## Product/Profile Scope
Source evidence used to continue the generic PHP compiler from wordpresshx-port.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a clean detached worktree at commit 7fdda0aa5ea66900819842aefeac6747421e9130.
2. Run npm ci --ignore-scripts.
3. Run npm run wphx:php:adoption-ci:check.
4. Run the five directly relevant core, array, callable, member, and private-emitter check lanes.
5. Observe that committed manifests hash an older WphxPhpCompiler.hx than the source at the same commit.

## Observed Failure
The aggregate WPHX PHP adoption check and five relevant check-mode lanes reject committed manifests because they hash an older WphxPhpCompiler.hx than the source at the same commit.

## Evidence
The source file SHA-256 is f3d3b91024a9b3fc5450ef0790d0f111114397caf10d26823576dabb209da182. Regenerating evidence in a disposable worktree made the five semantic lanes and subsequent checks pass, but that is not a clean-check result and was not committed upstream.

## Required Resolution
Identify the source commit that introduced the drift, reconcile source-port manifests/receipts through that repository authority or select a later immutable clean commit, and replay the aggregate adoption gate from a clean worktree.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
Calling refreshed local evidence a clean upstream pass would overstate provenance and hide a broken committed gate.

## Stop Condition
Do not claim a clean source-port adoption gate or import additional mixed typed-AST lowering as proven by those stale receipts.
acceptance_criteria: Record an immutable source-port commit whose committed WPHX PHP adoption inputs are current. Run npm ci --ignore-scripts and npm run wphx:php:adoption-ci:check from a clean detached worktree. Preserve failure evidence if semantic regressions remain. Update the SDK compiler provenance/receipt to the reconciled identity without changing the user-owned source checkout.
close_reason: Pinned reviewed wordpresshx-port merge 20b9c974f141375b6cf191db6f25b115812e282c (tree 1de1d4869f8cea49ebebc9e54295057c62dee011); fresh detached npm ci, adoption 29/28/0, and receipt links 493/493 pass without regeneration; SDK provenance and historical failure evidence updated.

title: SDK-035: Establish classic Genes differential fixture lane
description:

design: Compile one SDK-owned shared Haxe/HXX public facade twice through the immutable Genes v1.36.3 pin: strict split-ESM TSX and classic split-ESM JS plus declarations. Reuse the SDK-032 compile-time HXX lowerer and exact React/WordPress element types, but compare only a bounded corpus: retained module/public contract, pure function/data behavior, SSR markup, mounted useState behavior, and a real click transition. Validate declarations with TypeScript 5.9.3 and skipLibCheck=false, execute both bundles in isolated Node 22.17.0 + jsdom/React 18 processes, record expected target-shape differences separately from byte/format differences, require byte-identical clean replay per lane, and fail on any transcript divergence. This is evidence for the named corpus, not a universal mode switch. No sibling checkout is a build input; provenance is pinned to the admitted Genes release.
acceptance_criteria:

- Compile/run both modes with exact pins.
- Record expected target-shape differences separately from semantic differences.
- Block G2 on unexplained runtime divergence.
notes: Verified the bounded same-source Haxe/HXX differential. Exact Genes 1.36.3 emits strict TSX and classic JS+d.ts from one retained facade; strict consumers, public contract inventory, pure data, React SSR, mounted useState, real click, four clean compiles, four deterministic bundles, and four isolated runtime processes pass. Generated tree 33fe5430a23c837d7fea3aa9771abd099413e9a02a8a80d97e2a163d6bff3bf4; strict bundle f0700b1584463fb1cb5b42f016b85ccb53ec67675d9a79c53f7da2f7c84470c2; classic bundle 558b0b4f7cec15ebee0f9387fef4d5c34669caf4802e02c711d057730a1086a6. Implementation 99ba71f42b1a3b9b7835b44321dc0fa81155a0d4 passed all 10 hosted jobs in run 29654043752; Haxe job 88105136917. No Genes change or PR.
close_reason: Same-source strict TSX/classic JS+d.ts contract, deterministic replay, strict consumers, SSR, mounted state, real click, and full hosted matrix verified with zero unexplained differences.

title: SDK-014: Implement profile diff and upgrade diagnostics
description:
## Product/Profile Scope
Read-only exact-profile/catalog comparison and upgrade-impact report.

## Source Authority

## Layer Owner

## Generated Artifacts Affected
Machine/human diffs for APIs, signatures, packages, handles, classifications, metadata, and evidence states.

## Real Runtime Evidence Required
Golden diff fixtures including breaking catalog corrections and JSON output validation.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
A diff implying range support or auto-rewriting breaking changes.

## Acceptance Commands/Receipts
Run the named compiler/schema/static/runtime/package checks in clean environments; record exact commands, immutable input/tool versions, output hashes, logs, and evidence-ledger IDs. Expected dependency refs: 013.

## Dependencies
Canonical blocking edges are represented directly in beads and must match PRD §27.2 plus applicable ADRs.

## Stop Condition
Keep output advisory until every compared profile has exact validated evidence.
acceptance_criteria:
- Report additions, removals, signature/classification/handle/metadata changes.
- Distinguish upstream changes from SDK catalog corrections.
- Provide deterministic `--json` and actionable human output.
- Never infer support across an untested range or auto-accept breaking changes.
notes:
Implementation design: extend the closed profile-v1 capability shape additively with an optional reviewed contract payload (native identity, signature shape plus non-heuristic authority and receipt, canonical metadata facts, dependency identities). Semantic validation permits that payload only at typed evidence or later and requires its receipts/sorted facts. Add a read-only exact-catalog diff whose authority is identical, changed upstream identity, or a direct additive SDK correction; same-upstream drift without correction ancestry fails closed. Emit deterministic validated JSON plus actionable human output, conservatively flag removals/signature/handle/metadata regressions, preserve explicit correction migration text, and state that comparison neither establishes range support nor rewrites source.
Implemented and verified on main commit 6557019b044aba1036837dce0073db1116ee79f0. Added the read-only exact-catalog CLI, closed content-digested JSON schema, actionable human rendering, typed-only reviewed contract payload with non-heuristic signature authority, canonical metadata/dependency rules, upstream-versus-direct-correction classification, and conservative migration impacts. Blocking edges and affected implementation beads are represented directly in beads.

The PRD makes this decision explicit before dependent implementation because weakening vanilla behavior or conflating SDK and port progress.

title: Prove exact-optional compatibility for the Gutenberg declaration graph
description: The exact wp70-release @wordpress/components 32.2.0 graph contains Ariakit 0.4.21 declarations that do not pass TypeScript 5.9.3 with exactOptionalPropertyTypes enabled. SDK-032 therefore checks generated user modules with exactOptionalPropertyTypes=true and the complete provider declaration graph with skipLibCheck=false plus exactOptionalPropertyTypes=false. Determine whether a profile-owned type normalization, a newer exact provider profile, or a generalized upstream correction can close that gap without handwritten broadening or coupling to a single fixture.
acceptance_criteria: Produce a minimal provider-only reproduction; classify the mismatch as provider, adoption, or generator-owned; keep skipLibCheck=false; add a deterministic declaration compatibility gate; enable exactOptionalPropertyTypes for the complete provider graph only if the exact declarations genuinely pass or an evidence-backed general correction is admitted; do not patch node_modules or add WordPress-specific Genes behavior.

title: Eliminate weak JSX marker evaluation temporaries in Genes
description: SDK-032 emits three internal any[] locals while Genes preserves evaluation order for its runtime-free JSX marker protocol. The SDK verifier proves no weak type reaches a public field, parameter, return, prop, event, ref, hook, or context boundary, so this does not block SDK-032. Investigate whether Genes can project a precise generic marker-plan type or erase the temporary annotation without losing evaluation order. Any change belongs in an isolated ../genes worktree, must use a generic fixture with no WordPress symbols, and must not be consumed here until the full upstream suite passes and a release is admitted.
acceptance_criteria: A generic Genes fixture reproduces the JSX prop/child marker temporaries; emitted strict TSX has no avoidable any/unknown at those internal locals; evaluation order and classic/strict output remain equivalent; PATH=<exact-node> yarn test:ci passes in an isolated worktree; the generalized change is reviewed through a Genes PR and immutable release before wordpresshx updates its pin. If precise removal is unsound, record the compiler-level reason and retain an exact bounded inventory.

title: CI: Disable detached Git maintenance in HXX lock verifier
description:
## Scope
The SDK-080 dependency-lock verifier creates short-lived Git repositories for exact commit/tree checks. Git may launch detached automatic maintenance after fetch; tempfile cleanup can then race with writes under .git/objects and fail with ENOTEMPTY even though identity verification passed.

## Evidence
Hosted run 29623890774, Haxe job 88024323539, failed in TemporaryDirectory cleanup with Directory not empty: objects after all prior Haxe/PHP steps passed.

## Implementation
Disable automatic GC/maintenance for each ephemeral repository before fetch, preserve exact commit/tree/tag checks, and add a source-level regression assertion for the subprocess configuration.

## Acceptance
Local HXX dependency-lock verification and repository gates pass. A fresh hosted Haxe job and full matrix pass without weakening network, digest, commit, tree, or tag validation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Ephemeral Git repositories disable automatic GC and maintenance before fetch.
- Exact artifact/commit/tree/tag checks remain unchanged.
- Local HXX and repository gates pass.
- Hosted Haxe job and complete matrix pass.
notes:
Root cause/fix (2026-07-18): Git fetch runs automatic maintenance by default and may detach it; the official Git fetch option --no-auto-maintenance (also documented as --no-auto-gc) disables that post-fetch process. Add the option to each ephemeral exact-source fetch and assert its presence in the constructed command. Do not ignore tempfile cleanup errors or weaken any identity check.
Verification (2026-07-18): commit 448bc60d29e20d39f8eb2c4ddf3d9bd282290bae keeps every commit/tree/tag identity check and adds Git fetch --no-auto-maintenance plus a source regression assertion. Local packages/hxx/scripts/test.sh and repository gates passed. Hosted repository workflow run 29624107200 passed all 10 jobs; Haxe job 88024958566 passed the exact HXX dependency-lock verifier and complete HXX suite.
close_reason: Fixed the ephemeral Git cleanup race without weakening source identity checks; local HXX/repository gates and hosted 10-job run 29624107200 all passed.

title: CI: Upgrade checkout action off deprecated Node 20 runtime
description:
## Product/Profile Scope
Repository-hosted CI action runtime only; no SDK compatibility or generated-artifact behavior.

## Source Authority
GitHub Actions hosted warning on run 29605790579 plus the official action release and immutable commit metadata selected during implementation.

## Layer Owner
Repository CI/security tooling.

## Generated Artifacts Affected
.github/workflows/repository.yml action pins and security-tooling assertions.

## Real Runtime Evidence Required
All hosted jobs run without the Node 20 deprecation annotation after a reviewed immutable action upgrade.

## Unsafe/Compatibility Risk
Using an unreviewed action commit, weakening SHA pinning, or changing checkout history/submodule behavior.

## Acceptance Commands/Receipts
Review the official action release/commit, update every exact SHA and policy assertion, run local security/repository gates, push directly to main, and record a green hosted run with no Node 20 action warning.

## Dependencies
Discovered during SDK-090 hosted evidence; independent of WordPress runtime behavior.

## Stop Condition
Do not replace the full SHA with a floating tag or suppress the hosted warning.
acceptance_criteria:
- Pin an official checkout action release by full reviewed commit SHA.
- Keep fetch-depth behavior explicit for the security job and default behavior unchanged elsewhere.
- Update repository security assertions and pass local hook/history scans.
- Record a fully green hosted run with no checkout Node 20 deprecation annotation.
notes:
Selection (2026-07-18): official latest release v7.0.0, published 2026-06-18, lightweight tag/commit 9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0, tree 2ddbcfc53840ba99a608fb2a72dd13c3ea7cb8dc, verified GitHub signature, package version 7.0.0/MIT/Node >=24, action runtime node24, unchanged MIT license blob a67dca8b4f65d6bd351f6b1e333ce2cd84d843a5. Upgrade every workflow use by full SHA. Preserve security fetch-depth: 0 and default fetch-depth 1 elsewhere. v7 also blocks unsafe fork checkout under pull_request_target/workflow_run by default; this repository uses push/pull_request/workflow_dispatch and does not enable its unsafe override.
Verification (2026-07-18): commits 14629f7dad55e23fdc08d111b6ccdd8250616815 and 448bc60d29e20d39f8eb2c4ddf3d9bd282290bae pin all 10 checkout uses to reviewed v7.0.0 commit 9c091bb21b7c1c1d1991bb908d89e4e9dddfe3e0. Security keeps fetch-depth 0; all other jobs retain default depth 1. Hosted run 29624107200 passed all 10 jobs, and a complete log scan found zero Node 20 deprecation warnings. Receipt: manifests/evidence/ci-checkout-node24.json.
close_reason: Upgraded all immutable checkout pins to official node24-backed v7.0.0, preserved fetch semantics, passed local security/repository gates, and recorded green hosted run 29624107200 with zero Node 20 warnings.
